an1.10:1.1: “I do not see any other single tangible that so seizes and remains upon a woman's mind as this, _bhikkhus_, namely, a man's tangible.
an1.10:1.2: A man's tangible, _bhikkhus_, seizes and remains upon a woman's mind.”
an1.10:1.4: The first chapter on forms and so on is complete.
an1.1:0.2: 1. The Chapter on Forms and So On
an1.1:1.2: at one time the _Bhagavā_ was dwelling at Sāvatthī, in Jeta's Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika's park.
an1.1:2.1: “I do not see any other single form that so seizes and remains upon a man's mind as this, _bhikkhus_, namely, a woman's form.
an1.1:2.2: A woman's form, _bhikkhus_, seizes and remains upon a man's mind.”
an1.2:1.1: “I do not see any other single sound that so seizes and remains upon a man's mind as this, _bhikkhus_, namely, a woman's sound.
an1.2:1.2: A woman's sound, _bhikkhus_, seizes and remains upon a man's mind.”
an1.3:1.1: “I do not see any other single fragrance that so seizes and remains upon a man's mind as this, _bhikkhus_, namely, a woman's fragrance.
an1.3:1.2: A woman's fragrance, _bhikkhus_, seizes and remains upon a man's mind.”
an1.4:1.1: “I do not see any other single taste that so seizes and remains upon a man's mind as this, _bhikkhus_, namely, a woman's taste.
an1.4:1.2: A woman's taste, _bhikkhus_, seizes and remains upon a man's mind.”
an1.5:1.1: “I do not see any other single tangible that so seizes and remains upon a man's mind as this, _bhikkhus_, namely, a woman's tangible.
an1.5:1.2: A woman's tangible, _bhikkhus_, seizes and remains upon a man's mind.”
an1.6:1.1: “I do not see any other single form that so seizes and remains upon a woman's mind as this, _bhikkhus_, namely, a man's form.
an1.6:1.2: A man's form, _bhikkhus_, seizes and remains upon a woman's mind.”
an1.7:1.1: “I do not see any other single sound that so seizes and remains upon a woman's mind as this, _bhikkhus_, namely, a man's sound.
an1.7:1.2: A man's sound, _bhikkhus_, seizes and remains upon a woman's mind.”
an1.8:1.1: “I do not see any other single fragrance that so seizes and remains upon a woman's mind as this, _bhikkhus_, namely, a man's fragrance.
an1.8:1.2: A man's fragrance, _bhikkhus_, seizes and remains upon a woman's mind.”
an1.9:1.1: “I do not see any other single taste that so seizes and remains upon a woman's mind as this, _bhikkhus_, namely, a man's taste.
an1.9:1.2: A man's taste, _bhikkhus_, seizes and remains upon a woman's mind.”
